question: Does chromatin accessibility add INCREMENTAL value over the CRISOT sequence score for nominating off-targets? (v6.10.4)
data: GUIDE-seq (cell-based), 12 guides (>=5 actives), off-targets mapped to hg38, CRISOT-Score + cell-type-matched HEK293T DNase accessibility per off-target
method: (A) conditional logistic regression active ~ z(CRISOT) + z(accessibility), bootstrap-over-guides 95% CI on the accessibility coefficient. (B) leave-one-guide-out held-out AUPRC of CRISOT-only vs a CRISOT+accessibility logistic combiner, bootstrap 95% CI on the per-guide gap. Tested at two candidate imbalances.
verdict: Accessibility carries a SMALL, REAL conditional signal (coef ~0.35, CI excludes 0 at BOTH imbalances) but adds NO incremental ranking value over the CRISOT sequence score (held-out AUPRC gap CI includes 0 at both imbalances).
decision: Chromatin stays a VALIDATED ANNOTATION, NOT a re-ranker. The CRISOT sequence score already captures essentially all the practically-relevant nomination signal; a CRISOT+accessibility combiner does NOT improve held-out ranking, so it is NOT wired into the numeric risk score. The fitted combiner coefficients are recorded below for transparency but are intentionally not applied.

recorded_combiner_not_applied.reason: no demonstrated held-out ranking improvement over CRISOT alone
